1→package org.wordpress.android.ui.posts
  2→
  3→import androidx.annotation.MainThread
  4→import androidx.lifecycle.LiveData
  5→import androidx.lifecycle.MutableLiveData
  6→import kotlinx.coroutines.CoroutineDispatcher
  7→import kotlinx.coroutines.CoroutineScope

 12→import org.wordpress.android.fluxc.model.MediaModel
 13→import org.wordpress.android.fluxc.model.PostImmutableModel
 14→import org.wordpress.android.fluxc.model.PostModel
 15→import org.wordpress.android.fluxc.model.SiteModel
 16→import org.wordpress.android.fluxc.model.post.PostLocation
 17→import org.wordpress.android.fluxc.model.post.PostStatus
 18→import org.wordpress.android.fluxc.model.post.PostStatus.DRAFT
 19→import org.wordpress.android.fluxc.model.post.PostStatus.fromPost
 20→import org.wordpress.android.fluxc.store.PostStore
 21→import org.wordpress.android.modules.BG_THREAD
 22→import org.wordpress.android.modules.UI_THREAD
 23→import org.wordpress.android.ui.posts.EditPostRepository.UpdatePostResult.NoChanges
 24→import org.wordpress.android.ui.posts.EditPostRepository.UpdatePostResult.Updated
 25→import org.wordpress.android.ui.uploads.UploadService
 26→import org.wordpress.android.util.AppLog
 27→import org.wordpress.android.util.AppLog.T
 28→import org.wordpress.android.util.DateTimeUtils
 29→import org.wordpress.android.util.LocaleManagerWrapper
 30→import org.wordpress.android.viewmodel.Event
 31→import java.util.Arrays
 32→import javax.inject.Inject
 33→import javax.inject.Named
 34→import kotlin.coroutines.CoroutineContext
 35→
 36→private const val EMPTY_STRING = ""
 37→
 38→class EditPostRepository
 39→@Inject constructor(
 40→    private val localeManagerWrapper: LocaleManagerWrapper,
 41→    private val postStore: PostStore,
 42→    private val postUtils: PostUtilsWrapper,
 43→    @Named(UI_THREAD) private val mainDispatcher: CoroutineDispatcher,
 44→    @Named(BG_THREAD) private val bgDispatcher: CoroutineDispatcher
 45→) : CoroutineScope {
 46→    private var job: Job = Job()
 47→
 48→    override val coroutineContext: CoroutineContext
 49→        get() = mainDispatcher + job
 50→
 51→    private var post: PostModel? = null
 52→    private var postForUndo: PostModel? = null
 53→    private var postSnapshotWhenEditorOpened: PostModel? = null
 54→    private var postSnapshot: PostModel? = null
 55→    val id: Int
 56→        get() = post!!.id
 57→    val localSiteId: Int
 58→        get() = post!!.localSiteId
 59→    val remotePostId: Long
 60→        get() = post!!.remotePostId
 61→    val title: String
 62→        get() = post!!.title
 63→    val autoSaveTitle: String?
 64→        get() = post!!.autoSaveTitle
 65→    val content: String
 66→        get() = post!!.content
 67→    val autoSaveContent: String?
 68→        get() = post!!.autoSaveContent
 69→    val excerpt: String
 70→        get() = post!!.excerpt
 71→    val autoSaveExcerpt: String?
 72→        get() = post!!.autoSaveExcerpt
 73→    val password: String
 74→        get() = post!!.password
 75→    val status: PostStatus
 76→        get() = fromPost(getPost())
 77→    val isPage: Boolean
 78→        get() = post!!.isPage
 79→    val isLocalDraft: Boolean
 80→        get() = post!!.isLocalDraft
 81→    val isLocallyChanged: Boolean
 82→        get() = post!!.isLocallyChanged
 83→    val featuredImageId: Long
 84→        get() = post!!.featuredImageId
 85→    val dateCreated: String
 86→        get() = post!!.dateCreated
 87→    val changesConfirmedContentHashcode: Int
 88→        get() = post!!.changesConfirmedContentHashcode
 89→    val postFormat: String
 90→        get() = post!!.postFormat
 91→    val slug: String
 92→        get() = post!!.slug
 93→    val link: String
 94→        get() = post!!.link
 95→    val location: PostLocation
 96→        get() = post!!.location
 97→    val tagNameList: List<String>
 98→        get() = post!!.tagNameList
 99→    val dateLocallyChanged: String
100→        get() = post!!.dateLocallyChanged
101→    private var parent: PostModel? = null
102→
103→    private var locked = false
104→
105→    private val _postChanged = MutableLiveData<Event<PostImmutableModel>>()
106→    val postChanged: LiveData<Event<PostImmutableModel>> = _postChanged
107→
108→    @MainThread
109→    fun <T> update(action: (PostModel) -> T): T {
110→        reportTransactionState(true)
111→        val result = action(requireNotNull(post))
112→        reportTransactionState(false)
113→        _postChanged.value = Event(requireNotNull(post))
114→        return result
115→    }
116→
117→    fun updateAsync(
118→        action: (PostModel) -> Boolean,
119→        onCompleted: ((PostImmutableModel, UpdatePostResult) -> Unit)? = null
120→    ) {
121→        launch {
122→            reportTransactionState(true)
123→            val isUpdated = withContext(bgDispatcher) {
124→                action(requireNotNull(post))
125→            }
126→            reportTransactionState(false)
127→            if (isUpdated) {
128→                requireNotNull(post).let {
129→                    _postChanged.value = Event(it)
130→                }
131→            }
132→            onCompleted?.invoke(requireNotNull(post), if (isUpdated) Updated else NoChanges)
133→        }
134→    }
135→
136→    fun replace(action: (PostModel) -> PostModel) {
137→        reportTransactionState(true)
138→        this.post = action(requireNotNull(post))
139→        reportTransactionState(false)
140→    }
141→
142→    fun set(action: () -> PostModel) {
143→        reportTransactionState(true)
144→        this.post = action()
145→        reportTransactionState(false)
146→    }
147→
148→    @Synchronized
149→    private fun reportTransactionState(lock: Boolean) {
150→        if (lock && locked) {
151→            val message = "EditPostRepository: Transaction is writing on a locked thread ${
152→                Arrays.toString(
153→                    Thread.currentThread().stackTrace
154→                )
155→            }"
156→            AppLog.e(T.EDITOR, message)
157→        }
158→        locked = lock
159→    }
160→
161→    fun hasLocation() = requireNotNull(post).hasLocation()
162→
163→    fun hasPost() = post != null
164→    fun getPost(): PostImmutableModel? = post
165→    fun getEditablePost() = post
166→
167→    fun getPostForUndo() = postForUndo
168→
169→    fun hasStatus(status: PostStatus): Boolean {
170→        return post?.status == status.toString()
171→    }
172→
173→    fun getPendingMediaForPost(): Set<MediaModel> =
174→        UploadService.getPendingMediaForPost(post)
175→
176→    fun getPendingOrInProgressMediaUploadsForPost(): List<MediaModel> =
177→        UploadService.getPendingOrInProgressMediaUploadsForPost(post)
178→
179→    fun updatePublishDateIfShouldBePublishedImmediately(post: PostModel) {
180→        if (postUtils.shouldPublishImmediately(fromPost(post), post.dateCreated)) {
181→            post.setDateCreated(DateTimeUtils.iso8601FromDate(localeManagerWrapper.getCurrentCalendar().time))
182→        }
183→    }
184→
185→    fun isPostPublishable() = post?.let { postUtils.isPublishable(it) } ?: false
186→
187→    fun saveForUndo() {
188→        postForUndo = post?.clone()
189→    }
190→
191→    fun undo() {
192→        this.post = postForUndo?.clone()
193→    }
194→
195→    fun postHasChanges(): Boolean {
196→        checkNotNull(postSnapshot) { "Post snapshot cannot be null at this point" }
197→        return post != postSnapshot ||
198→                post?.changesConfirmedContentHashcode != postSnapshot?.changesConfirmedContentHashcode
199→    }
200→
201→    fun savePostSnapshot() {
202→        postSnapshot = post?.clone()
203→    }
204→
205→    fun savePostSnapshotWhenEditorOpened() {
206→        postSnapshotWhenEditorOpened = post?.clone()
207→    }
208→
209→    fun hasPostSnapshotWhenEditorOpened() = postSnapshotWhenEditorOpened != null
210→
211→    fun updateStatusFromPostSnapshotWhenEditorOpened() {
212→        // the user has just tapped on "PUBLISH" on an empty post, make sure to set the status back to the
213→        // original post's status as we could not proceed with the action
214→        reportTransactionState(true)
215→        requireNotNull(post).setStatus(postSnapshotWhenEditorOpened?.status ?: DRAFT.toString())
216→        reportTransactionState(false)
217→    }
218→
219→    fun postWasChangedInCurrentSession() = postUtils.postHasEdits(
220→        postSnapshotWhenEditorOpened,
221→        requireNotNull(post)
222→    )
223→
224→    fun loadPostByLocalPostId(postId: Int) {
225→        reportTransactionState(true)
226→        post = postStore.getPostByLocalPostId(postId)
227→        savePostSnapshot()
228→        reportTransactionState(false)
229→    }
230→
231→    fun loadPostByRemotePostId(remotePostId: Long, site: SiteModel) {
232→        reportTransactionState(true)
233→        post = postStore.getPostByRemotePostId(remotePostId, site)
234→        savePostSnapshot()
235→        reportTransactionState(false)
236→    }
237→
238→    fun getParentTitle(site: SiteModel): String {
239→        // update parent local field if parent ID of current post has changed
240→        if (parent?.remotePostId != post?.parentId) {
241→            runBlocking {
242→                parent = post?.parentId
243→                    ?.takeUnless { it == 0L }
244→                    ?.let { postStore.getPostByRemotePostId(it, site) }
245→            }
246→        }
247→
248→        return parent?.title ?: EMPTY_STRING
249→    }
250→
251→    sealed class UpdatePostResult {
252→        object Updated : UpdatePostResult()
253→        object NoChanges : UpdatePostResult()
254→    }
255→}
256→

1→package org.wordpress.android.ui.posts
  2→
  3→import android.text.TextUtils
  4→import androidx.lifecycle.LiveData
  5→import androidx.lifecycle.MutableLiveData
  6→import androidx.lifecycle.ViewModel
  7→import org.wordpress.android.R
  8→import org.wordpress.android.fluxc.model.PostImmutableModel
  9→import org.wordpress.android.fluxc.model.post.PostStatus
 10→import org.wordpress.android.fluxc.model.post.PostStatus.DRAFT
 11→import org.wordpress.android.fluxc.model.post.PostStatus.PUBLISHED
 12→import org.wordpress.android.fluxc.model.post.PostStatus.SCHEDULED
 13→import org.wordpress.android.fluxc.store.PostSchedulingNotificationStore
 14→import org.wordpress.android.fluxc.store.PostSchedulingNotificationStore.SchedulingReminderModel.Period
 15→import org.wordpress.android.fluxc.store.PostSchedulingNotificationStore.SchedulingReminderModel.Period.OFF
 16→import org.wordpress.android.fluxc.store.PostSchedulingNotificationStore.SchedulingReminderModel.Period.ONE_HOUR
 17→import org.wordpress.android.fluxc.store.PostSchedulingNotificationStore.SchedulingReminderModel.Period.TEN_MINUTES
 18→import org.wordpress.android.fluxc.store.PostSchedulingNotificationStore.SchedulingReminderModel.Period.WHEN_PUBLISHED
 19→import org.wordpress.android.fluxc.store.SiteStore
 20→import org.wordpress.android.ui.posts.EditPostRepository.UpdatePostResult
 21→import org.wordpress.android.util.DateTimeUtils
 22→import org.wordpress.android.util.LocaleManagerWrapper
 23→import org.wordpress.android.viewmodel.Event
 24→import org.wordpress.android.viewmodel.ResourceProvider
 25→import java.util.Calendar
 26→
 27→abstract class PublishSettingsViewModel
 28→constructor(
 29→    private val resourceProvider: ResourceProvider,
 30→    private val postSettingsUtils: PostSettingsUtils,
 31→    private val localeManagerWrapper: LocaleManagerWrapper,
 32→    private val postSchedulingNotificationStore: PostSchedulingNotificationStore,
 33→    private val siteStore: SiteStore
 34→) : ViewModel() {
 35→    protected var editPostRepository: EditPostRepository? = null
 36→        private set
 37→
 38→    var canPublishImmediately: Boolean = false
 39→
 40→    var year: Int? = null
 41→        private set
 42→    var month: Int? = null
 43→        private set
 44→    var day: Int? = null
 45→        private set
 46→    var hour: Int? = null
 47→        private set
 48→    var minute: Int? = null
 49→        private set
 50→
 51→    private val _onDatePicked = MutableLiveData<Event<Unit>>()
 52→    val onDatePicked: LiveData<Event<Unit>> = _onDatePicked
 53→    private val _onPublishedDateChanged = MutableLiveData<Event<Calendar>>()
 54→    val onPublishedDateChanged: LiveData<Event<Calendar>> = _onPublishedDateChanged
 55→    private val _onPostStatusChanged = MutableLiveData<PostStatus>()
 56→    val onPostStatusChanged: LiveData<PostStatus> = _onPostStatusChanged
 57→    private val _onUiModel = MutableLiveData<PublishUiModel>()
 58→    val onUiModel: LiveData<PublishUiModel> = _onUiModel
 59→
 60→    @Suppress("VariableNaming")
 61→    protected val _onToast = MutableLiveData<Event<String>>()
 62→    val onToast: LiveData<Event<String>> = _onToast
 63→    private val _onShowNotificationDialog = MutableLiveData<Event<Period?>>()
 64→    val onShowNotificationDialog: LiveData<Event<Period?>> = _onShowNotificationDialog
 65→    private val _onNotificationTime = MutableLiveData<Period?>()
 66→    val onNotificationTime: LiveData<Period?> = _onNotificationTime
 67→    private val _onNotificationAdded = MutableLiveData<Event<Notification>>()
 68→    val onNotificationAdded: LiveData<Event<Notification>> = _onNotificationAdded
 69→    private val _onAddToCalendar = MutableLiveData<Event<CalendarEvent>>()
 70→    val onAddToCalendar: LiveData<Event<CalendarEvent>> = _onAddToCalendar
 71→
 72→    open fun start(postRepository: EditPostRepository?) {
 73→        editPostRepository = postRepository
 74→        val startCalendar = postRepository?.let { getCurrentPublishDateAsCalendar(it) }
 75→            ?: localeManagerWrapper.getCurrentCalendar()
 76→        updateDateAndTimeFromCalendar(startCalendar)
 77→        onPostStatusChanged(postRepository?.getPost())
 78→    }
 79→
 80→    fun onPostStatusChanged(postModel: PostImmutableModel?) {
 81→        canPublishImmediately = postModel?.let {
 82→            PostUtils.shouldPublishImmediatelyOptionBeAvailable(
 83→                it.status
 84→            )
 85→        } ?: false
 86→        updateUiModel(postModel = postModel)
 87→    }
 88→
 89→    fun publishNow() {
 90→        val currentCalendar = localeManagerWrapper.getCurrentCalendar()
 91→        updateDateAndTimeFromCalendar(currentCalendar)
 92→        _onPublishedDateChanged.postValue(Event(currentCalendar))
 93→    }
 94→
 95→    fun onTimeSelected(selectedHour: Int, selectedMinute: Int) {
 96→        this.hour = selectedHour
 97→        this.minute = selectedMinute
 98→        val calendar = localeManagerWrapper.getCurrentCalendar()
 99→        calendar.set(year!!, month!!, day!!, hour!!, minute!!)
100→        _onPublishedDateChanged.postValue(Event(calendar))
101→    }
102→
103→    fun onDateSelected(year: Int, month: Int, dayOfMonth: Int) {
104→        this.year = year
105→        this.month = month
106→        this.day = dayOfMonth
107→        _onDatePicked.postValue(Event(Unit))
108→    }
109→
110→    fun onShowDialog(postModel: PostImmutableModel) {
111→        if (areNotificationsEnabled(postModel)) {
112→            val currentPeriod = postSchedulingNotificationStore.getSchedulingReminderPeriod(postModel.id)
113→            _onShowNotificationDialog.postValue(Event(currentPeriod))
114→        } else {
115→            _onToast.postValue(Event(resourceProvider.getString(R.string.post_notification_error)))
116→        }
117→    }
118→
119→    fun updatePost(updatedDate: Calendar, postRepository: EditPostRepository?) {
120→        postRepository?.updateAsync({ postModel ->
121→            val dateCreated = DateTimeUtils.iso8601FromDate(updatedDate.time)
122→            postModel.setDateCreated(dateCreated)
123→            val initialPostStatus = postRepository.status
124→            val isPublishDateInTheFuture = PostUtils.isPublishDateInTheFuture(dateCreated)
125→            var finalPostStatus = initialPostStatus
126→            if (initialPostStatus == DRAFT && isPublishDateInTheFuture) {
127→                // The previous logic was setting the status twice, once from draft to published and when the user
128→                // picked the time, it set it from published to scheduled. This is now done in one step.
129→                finalPostStatus = SCHEDULED
130→            } else if (initialPostStatus == PUBLISHED && postRepository.isLocalDraft) {
131→                // if user was changing dates for a local draft (not saved yet), only way to have it set to PUBLISH
132→                // is by running into the if case above. So, if they're updating the date again by calling
133→                // `updatePublishDate()`, get it back to DRAFT.
134→                finalPostStatus = DRAFT
135→            } else if (initialPostStatus == SCHEDULED && !isPublishDateInTheFuture) {
136→                // if this is a SCHEDULED post and the user is trying to Back-date it now, let's update it to DRAFT.
137→                // The other option was to make it published immediately but, let the user actively do that rather than
138→                // having the app be smart about it - we don't want to accidentally publish a post.
139→                finalPostStatus = DRAFT
140→                // show toast only once, when time is shown
141→                _onToast.postValue(Event(resourceProvider.getString(R.string.editor_post_converted_back_to_draft)))
142→            }
143→            postModel.setStatus(finalPostStatus.toString())
144→            _onPostStatusChanged.postValue(finalPostStatus)
145→            val scheduledTime = postSchedulingNotificationStore.getSchedulingReminderPeriod(postRepository.id)
146→            updateNotifications(postRepository, scheduledTime)
147→            true
148→        }, onCompleted = { postModel, result ->
149→            if (result == UpdatePostResult.Updated) {
150→                updateUiModel(postModel = postModel)
151→            }
152→        })
153→    }
154→
155→    fun updateUiModel(postModel: PostImmutableModel?) {
156→        if (postModel != null) {
157→            val notificationTime = postSchedulingNotificationStore.getSchedulingReminderPeriod(postModel.id)
158→            val publishDateLabel = postSettingsUtils.getPublishDateLabel(postModel)
159→            val now = localeManagerWrapper.getCurrentCalendar().timeInMillis - 10000
160→            val dateCreated = (DateTimeUtils.dateFromIso8601(postModel.dateCreated)
161→                ?: localeManagerWrapper.getCurrentCalendar().time).time
162→            val enableNotification = areNotificationsEnabled(postModel)
163→            val showNotification = dateCreated > now
164→            val notificationLabel = if (enableNotification && showNotification) {
165→                notificationTime.toLabel()
166→            } else {
167→                R.string.post_notification_off
168→            }
169→            _onUiModel.value = PublishUiModel(
170→                publishDateLabel,
171→                notificationLabel = notificationLabel,
172→                notificationEnabled = enableNotification,
173→                notificationVisible = showNotification
174→            )
175→        } else {
176→            _onUiModel.value = PublishUiModel(resourceProvider.getString(R.string.immediately))
177→        }
178→    }
179→
180→    fun onNotificationCreated(scheduleTime: Period?) {
181→        _onNotificationTime.value = scheduleTime
182→    }
183→
184→    fun scheduleNotification(postRepository: EditPostRepository, notificationTime: Period) {
185→        updateNotifications(postRepository, notificationTime)
186→        updateUiModel(postRepository.getPost())
187→    }
188→
189→    fun onAddToCalendar(postRepository: EditPostRepository) {
190→        val startTime = DateTimeUtils.dateFromIso8601(postRepository.dateCreated).time
191→        val site = siteStore.getSiteByLocalId(postRepository.localSiteId)
192→        val title = resourceProvider.getString(
193→            R.string.calendar_scheduled_post_title,
194→            postRepository.title
195→        )
196→        val appName = resourceProvider.getString(R.string.app_name)
197→        val description = resourceProvider.getString(
198→            R.string.calendar_scheduled_post_description,
199→            postRepository.title,
200→            site?.name ?: site?.url ?: "",
201→            appName,
202→            postRepository.link
203→        )
204→        _onAddToCalendar.value = Event(CalendarEvent(title, description, startTime))
205→    }
206→
207→    private fun getCurrentPublishDateAsCalendar(postRepository: EditPostRepository): Calendar {
208→        val calendar = localeManagerWrapper.getCurrentCalendar()
209→        val dateCreated = postRepository.dateCreated
210→        // Set the currently selected time if available
211→        if (!TextUtils.isEmpty(dateCreated)) {
212→            calendar.time = DateTimeUtils.dateFromIso8601(dateCreated)
213→            calendar.timeZone = localeManagerWrapper.getTimeZone()
214→        }
215→        return calendar
216→    }
217→
218→    private fun updateNotifications(
219→        postRepository: EditPostRepository,
220→        schedulingReminderPeriod: Period = OFF
221→    ) {
222→        postSchedulingNotificationStore.deleteSchedulingReminders(postRepository.id)
223→        if (schedulingReminderPeriod != OFF) {
224→            val notificationId = postSchedulingNotificationStore.schedule(postRepository.id, schedulingReminderPeriod)
225→            val scheduledCalendar = localeManagerWrapper.getCurrentCalendar().apply {
226→                timeInMillis = System.currentTimeMillis()
227→                time = DateTimeUtils.dateFromIso8601(postRepository.dateCreated)
228→                val scheduledMinutes = when (schedulingReminderPeriod) {
229→                    ONE_HOUR -> -60
230→                    TEN_MINUTES -> -10
231→                    WHEN_PUBLISHED -> 0
232→                    OFF -> return
233→                }
234→                add(Calendar.MINUTE, scheduledMinutes)
235→            }
236→            if (scheduledCalendar.after(localeManagerWrapper.getCurrentCalendar())) {
237→                notificationId?.let {
238→                    _onNotificationAdded.postValue(Event(Notification(notificationId, scheduledCalendar.timeInMillis)))
239→                }
240→            }
241→        }
242→    }
243→
244→    private fun areNotificationsEnabled(postModel: PostImmutableModel): Boolean {
245→        val futureTime = localeManagerWrapper.getCurrentCalendar().timeInMillis + 6000
246→        val dateCreated = (DateTimeUtils.dateFromIso8601(postModel.dateCreated)
247→            ?: localeManagerWrapper.getCurrentCalendar().time).time
248→        return dateCreated > futureTime
249→    }
250→
251→    private fun Period.toLabel(): Int {
252→        return when (this) {
253→            OFF -> R.string.post_notification_off
254→            ONE_HOUR -> R.string.post_notification_one_hour_before
255→            TEN_MINUTES -> R.string.post_notification_ten_minutes_before
256→            WHEN_PUBLISHED -> R.string.post_notification_when_published
257→        }
258→    }
259→
260→    private fun updateDateAndTimeFromCalendar(startCalendar: Calendar) {
261→        year = startCalendar.get(Calendar.YEAR)
262→        month = startCalendar.get(Calendar.MONTH)
263→        day = startCalendar.get(Calendar.DAY_OF_MONTH)
264→        hour = startCalendar.get(Calendar.HOUR_OF_DAY)
265→        minute = startCalendar.get(Calendar.MINUTE)
266→    }
267→
268→    data class PublishUiModel(
269→        val publishDateLabel: String,
270→        val notificationLabel: Int = R.string.post_notification_off,
271→        val notificationEnabled: Boolean = false,
272→        val notificationVisible: Boolean = true
273→    )
274→
275→    data class Notification(val id: Int, val scheduledTime: Long)
276→
277→    data class CalendarEvent(val title: String, val description: String, val startTime: Long)
278→}
279→

1→package org.wordpress.android.ui.posts
 2→
 3→import androidx.lifecycle.LiveData
 4→import androidx.lifecycle.MutableLiveData
 5→import org.wordpress.android.R
 6→import org.wordpress.android.fluxc.model.SiteModel
 7→import org.wordpress.android.fluxc.store.PostSchedulingNotificationStore
 8→import org.wordpress.android.fluxc.store.SiteStore
 9→import org.wordpress.android.models.Person
10→import org.wordpress.android.ui.people.utils.PeopleUtils.FetchUsersCallback
11→import org.wordpress.android.ui.people.utils.PeopleUtilsWrapper
12→import org.wordpress.android.util.LocaleManagerWrapper
13→import org.wordpress.android.viewmodel.Event
14→import org.wordpress.android.viewmodel.ResourceProvider
15→import javax.inject.Inject
16→
17→class EditPostPublishSettingsViewModel @Inject constructor(
18→    private val resourceProvider: ResourceProvider,
19→    postSettingsUtils: PostSettingsUtils,
20→    private val peopleUtilsWrapper: PeopleUtilsWrapper,
21→    localeManagerWrapper: LocaleManagerWrapper,
22→    postSchedulingNotificationStore: PostSchedulingNotificationStore,
23→    private val siteStore: SiteStore,
24→) : PublishSettingsViewModel(
25→    resourceProvider,
26→    postSettingsUtils,
27→    localeManagerWrapper,
28→    postSchedulingNotificationStore,
29→    siteStore
30→) {
31→    private val _authors = MutableLiveData<List<Person>>()
32→    val authors: LiveData<List<Person>> = _authors
33→
34→    // Used for combining fetched users
35→    private val fetchedAuthors = mutableListOf<Person>()
36→
37→    private var isStarted = false
38→
39→    override fun start(postRepository: EditPostRepository?) {
40→        super.start(postRepository)
41→        if (isStarted) return
42→        isStarted = true
43→
44→        val siteModel = postRepository?.localSiteId?.let {
45→            siteStore.getSiteByLocalId(it)
46→        } ?: return
47→
48→        if (siteModel.hasCapabilityListUsers) {
49→            fetchAuthors(siteModel)
50→        }
51→    }
52→
53→    // This fetches authors page by page and combine the result in fetchedAuthors.
54→    private fun fetchAuthors(site: SiteModel) {
55→        peopleUtilsWrapper.fetchAuthors(site, fetchedAuthors.size, object : FetchUsersCallback {
56→            override fun onSuccess(peopleList: List<Person>, isEndOfList: Boolean) {
57→                fetchedAuthors.addAll(peopleList)
58→                if (isEndOfList) {
59→                    _authors.value = fetchedAuthors
60→                } else {
61→                    fetchAuthors(site)
62→                }
63→            }
64→
65→            override fun onError() {
66→                _onToast.postValue(Event(resourceProvider.getString(R.string.error_fetch_authors_list)))
67→            }
68→        })
69→    }
70→
71→    fun getAuthorIndex(authorId: Long) = authors.value?.indexOfFirst { it.personID == authorId } ?: -1
72→}
73→

157→        buildConfigField "boolean", "ENABLE_ADD_SELF_HOSTED_SITE", "true"
158→        buildConfigField "boolean", "ENABLE_SIGNUP", "true"
159→        buildConfigField "boolean", "ENABLE_READER", "true"
160→        buildConfigField "boolean", "ENABLE_CREATE_FAB", "true"
161→        buildConfigField "boolean", "ENABLE_FOLLOWED_SITES_SETTINGS", "true"
162→        buildConfigField "boolean", "ENABLE_WHATS_NEW_FEATURE", "true"
163→        buildConfigField "boolean", "ENABLE_QRCODE_AUTH_FLOW", "true"
164→        buildConfigField "boolean", "ENABLE_OPEN_WEB_LINKS_WITH_JP_FLOW", "true"
165→        buildConfigField "boolean", "BLAZE_MANAGE_CAMPAIGNS", "false"
166→        buildConfigField "boolean", "DASHBOARD_PERSONALIZATION", "false"
167→        buildConfigField "boolean", "ENABLE_SITE_MONITORING", "false"
168→        buildConfigField "boolean", "SYNC_PUBLISHING", "false"
169→
170→        manifestPlaceholders = [magicLinkScheme:"wordpress"]
171→    }
172→
173→    // Gutenberg's dependency - react-native-video is using
174→    // Java API 1.8
175→    compileOptions {

177→        coreLibraryDesugaringEnabled true
178→        sourceCompatibility JavaVersion.VERSION_1_8
179→        targetCompatibility JavaVersion.VERSION_1_8

182→    flavorDimensions "app", "buildType"

184→    productFlavors {

186→            isDefault true
187→            dimension "app"
188→
189→            applicationId "org.wordpress.android"
190→            buildConfigField "boolean", "IS_JETPACK_APP", "false"
191→            buildConfigField "String", "TRACKS_EVENT_PREFIX", '"wpandroid_"'
192→            buildConfigField "String", "PUSH_NOTIFICATIONS_APP_KEY", '"org.wordpress.android"'
193→            buildConfigField "boolean", "ENABLE_QRCODE_AUTH_FLOW", "false"
194→            buildConfigField "boolean", "ENABLE_OPEN_WEB_LINKS_WITH_JP_FLOW", "true"
195→
196→            manifestPlaceholders = [magicLinkScheme:"wordpress"]
197→        }
198→
199→        jetpack {
200→            dimension "app"
201→
202→            applicationId "com.jetpack.android"
203→            buildConfigField "boolean", "IS_JETPACK_APP", "true"
204→            buildConfigField "boolean", "ENABLE_SITE_CREATION", "true"
205→            buildConfigField "boolean", "ENABLE_ADD_SELF_HOSTED_SITE", "true"
206→            buildConfigField "boolean", "ENABLE_SIGNUP", "true"
207→            buildConfigField "boolean", "ENABLE_READER", "true"
208→            buildConfigField "boolean", "ENABLE_CREATE_FAB", "true"
209→            buildConfigField "boolean", "ENABLE_FOLLOWED_SITES_SETTINGS", "true"
210→            buildConfigField "boolean", "ENABLE_WHATS_NEW_FEATURE", "true"
211→            buildConfigField "String", "TRACKS_EVENT_PREFIX", '"jpandroid_"'
212→            buildConfigField "String", "PUSH_NOTIFICATIONS_APP_KEY", '"com.jetpack.android"'
213→            buildConfigField "boolean", "ENABLE_QRCODE_AUTH_FLOW", "true"
214→            buildConfigField "boolean", "ENABLE_OPEN_WEB_LINKS_WITH_JP_FLOW", "false"
215→
216→            manifestPlaceholders = [magicLinkScheme:"jetpack"]
217→
218→            // Limit string resources to Mag16 only for Jetpack
219→            // Note 1: this only affects included _locales_; variants for `values-night`, `values-land` and other configs are _still_ preserved in Jetpack and not filtered by this.
220→            resourceConfigurations = ["ar", "de", "es", "fr", "he", "id", "it", "ja", "ko", "nl", "pt-rBR", "ru", "sv", "tr", "zh-rCN", "zh-rTW"]
221→            // Note 2: `in` is the legacy locale code for `id` and `iw` is the legacy locale code for `he`.
222→            // We need to include those to make sure users with older Android versions and still using those legacy codes still have those translations show up at runtime.
223→            resourceConfigurations += ["in", "iw"]
224→        }
225→
226→        // Used for release/beta testing builds. Usually shouldn't be build locally.
227→        // AppName: WordPress/Jetpack
228→        vanilla {
229→            dimension "buildType"
230→
231→            buildConfigField "boolean", "ME_ACTIVITY_AVAILABLE", "false"
232→            buildConfigField "long", "REMOTE_CONFIG_FETCH_INTERVAL", "3600"
233→            buildConfigField "boolean", "ENABLE_DEBUG_SETTINGS", "false"
234→        }
235→
236→        // Used for local development - preferred variant for developers.
237→        // AppName: WordPress Beta/Jetpack Beta
238→        wasabi {
239→            applicationIdSuffix ".beta"
240→            dimension "buildType"
241→        }
242→
243→        // Used for CI builds on PRs (aka "Prototype Builds"). Can be used locally when a developer needs to install multiple versions of the app on the same device.
244→        // AppName: WordPress Pre-Alpha/Jetpack Pre-Alpha
245→        jalapeno {
246→            isDefault true
247→            applicationIdSuffix ".prealpha"
248→            dimension "buildType"
249→        }
250→
251→        // Also dynamically add additional `buildConfigFields` to our app flavors from any `wp.`/`jp.`-prefixed property in `gradle.properties`
252→        def properties = loadPropertiesFromFile(checkGradlePropertiesFile())
253→        addBuildConfigFieldsFromPrefixedProperties(wordpress, properties, ['wp'])
254→        addBuildConfigFieldsFromPrefixedProperties(jetpack, properties, ['wp', 'jp']) // Inherit same properties used from WP first then overwrite with JP-specific ones
255→    }
256→
257→    buildTypes {
258→        release {
259→            // Proguard is used to shrink our apk, and reduce the number of methods in our final apk,
260→            // but we don't obfuscate the bytecode.
261→            minifyEnabled true
262→            proguardFiles getDefaultProguardFile('proguard-android.txt'), 'proguard.cfg'
263→        }
264→
265→        debug {
266→            minifyEnabled false
267→            buildConfigField "String", "APP_PN_KEY", "\"org.wordpress.android.debug.build\""
268→            pseudoLocalesEnabled true
269→        }
270→    }
271→
272→    testOptions {

274→        unitTests {
275→            includeAndroidResources = true
276→            returnDefaultValues = true
277→        }
278→    }
279→
280→    lint {
281→        warningsAsErrors = true
282→        checkDependencies = true
283→        checkGeneratedSources = true
284→        lintConfig file("${project.rootDir}/config/lint/lint.xml")
285→        baseline file("${project.rootDir}/config/lint/baseline.xml")
286→        enable += 'UnknownNullness'
287→    }
288→
289→    packagingOptions {
290→        // MPAndroidChart uses androidX - remove this line when we migrate everything to androidX
291→        exclude 'META-INF/proguard/androidx-annotations.pro'
292→
293→        // Exclude React Native's JSC and Fabric JNI
294→        exclude '**/libjscexecutor.so'
295→        exclude '**/libfabricjni.so'
296→
297→        // Avoid React Native's JNI duplicated classes
298→        pickFirst '**/libc++_shared.so'
299→        pickFirst '**/libfbjni.so'
300→
301→        pickFirst 'META-INF/-no-jdk.kotlin_module'
302→
303→    }
304→
305→    bundle {
306→        language {
307→            // Don't split language resources for App Bundles.
308→            // This is required to switch language in app.
309→            enableSplit = false
310→        }
311→    }
312→
313→    buildFeatures {
314→        buildConfig true
315→        viewBinding true
316→        compose true
317→
318→        composeOptions {
319→            kotlinCompilerExtensionVersion = androidxComposeCompilerVersion
320→        }
321→    }
322→}

324→/// Dynamically add `buildConfigFields` on a given variant/flavor from prefixed properties
325→/// (This is used to e.g. add every property prefixed `wp.` in `gradle.properties` as a BuildConfigField in the `wordpress` flavor)
326→///
327→/// `prefixes` is an array of prefixes to search for, without their `.` dot (e.g. `["wp", "jp"]`).
328→/// Properties found with prefixes first in that array will be overridden by the ones found with prefixes later in the array.
329→static def addBuildConfigFieldsFromPrefixedProperties(variant, properties, prefixes) {
330→    // Build a Map of prefixed properties found. Keys in this Map will be lowercased and `.` replaced by `_`
331→    def fields_map = [:]
332→    prefixes.each { prefix ->
333→        properties.each { property ->
334→            if (property.key.toLowerCase().startsWith("${prefix.toLowerCase()}.")) {
335→                def key = property.key.toLowerCase().replace("${prefix.toLowerCase()}.", "").replace(".", "_")
336→                fields_map[key] = property.value
337→            }
338→        }
339→    }
340→
341→    // Then define the found properties as buildConfigFields
342→    fields_map.each {
343→        variant.buildConfigField "String", it.key.toUpperCase(), "\"${it.value}\""
344→    }
345→}

601→    repositoryOwner = "wordpress-mobile"
602→    repositoryName = "WordPress-Android"
603→    pullRequestId = System.properties['GITHUB_PULLREQUESTID']
604→    username = System.properties['GITHUB_USERNAME']
605→    password = System.properties['GITHUB_PASSWORD']
606→    oAuth2Token = System.properties['GITHUB_OAUTH2TOKEN']
607→    gitHubUrl = "https://api.github.com/"
608→    createCommentWithAllSingleFileComments = false
609→    createSingleFileComments = true
610→    commentOnlyChangedContent = true
611→    minSeverity = SEVERITY.INFO //ERROR, INFO, WARN
612→    commentTemplate = """
613→**Reporter**: {{violation.reporter}}{{#violation.rule}}\n
614→**Rule**: {{violation.rule}}{{/violation.rule}}
615→**Severity**: {{violation.severity}}
616→**File**: {{violation.file}}:{{violation.startLine}}{{#violation.source}}
617→**Source**: {{violation.source}}{{/violation.source}}
618→{{violation.message}}
619→"""
620→    violations = [
621→            ["CHECKSTYLE", ".", ".*/build/.*/checkstyle/.*\\.xml\$", "CheckStyle"],
622→            ["CHECKSTYLE", ".", ".*/build/.*/detekt/.*\\.xml\$", "Detekt"]
623→    ]

633→    createSingleFileComments = false
634→    commentOnlyChangedContent = true
635→    commentOnlyChangedFiles = false
636→    minSeverity = SEVERITY.INFO
637→    commentTemplate = """
638→<details><summary>The PR caused some dependency changes (expand to see details)</summary>
639→<p>
640→
641→```diff
642→{{{violation.message}}}
643→```
644→
645→</p>
646→</details>
647→
648→*Please review and act accordingly*
649→"""
650→    violations = [
651→            ["GENERIC", ".", ".*/build/.*/diff/.*\\.txt\$", "DependenciesDiffChecker"],
652→    ]
653→}
654→
655→tasks.register("printVersionName") {
656→    doLast {
657→        println android.defaultConfig.versionName
658→    }
659→}
660→
661→tasks.register("printAllVersions") {
662→    doLast {
663→        android.applicationVariants.all { variant ->
664→            println "${variant.name}: ${variant.versionName} (${variant.versionCode})"
665→        }
666→    }
667→}
668→
669→tasks.register("printResourceConfigurations") {
670→    doLast {
671→        android.productFlavors.each { flavor ->
672→            if (flavor.dimension == "app") {
673→                println "${flavor.name}: ${flavor.resourceConfigurations}"
674→            }
675→        }
676→    }
677→}
678→
679→def checkGradlePropertiesFile() {
680→    def inputFile = file("${rootDir}/gradle.properties")
681→    if (!inputFile.exists()) {
682→        throw new StopActionException("Build configuration file gradle.properties doesn't exist, follow README instructions")
683→    }
684→    return inputFile
685→}
686→
687→static def loadPropertiesFromFile(inputFile) {
688→    def properties = new Properties()
689→    inputFile.withInputStream { stream ->
690→        properties.load(stream)
691→    }
692→    return properties
693→}
694→
695→// For app signing
696→if (["uploadStoreFile", "uploadStorePassword", "uploadKeyAlias", "uploadKeyPassword"].count { !project.hasProperty(it) } == 0) {
697→    android {
698→        signingConfigs {
699→            release {
700→                storeFile = rootProject.file(project.uploadStoreFile)
701→                storePassword = project.uploadStorePassword
702→                keyAlias = project.uploadKeyAlias
703→                keyPassword = project.uploadKeyPassword
704→            }
705→        }
706→    }
707→    android.buildTypes.release.signingConfig = android.signingConfigs.release
708→}
709→
710→if (project.hasProperty("debugStoreFile")) {
711→    def sharedDebugStore = file(project.debugStoreFile.replaceFirst("^~", System.getProperty("user.home")))
712→    if (sharedDebugStore.exists()) {
713→        android {
714→            signingConfigs {
715→                debug {
716→                    storeFile sharedDebugStore
717→                }
718→            }
719→        }
720→    }
721→}
722→
723→// Copy React Native JavaScript bundle and source map so they can be upload it to the Crash logging
724→// service during the build process.
725→android {
726→    applicationVariants.configureEach { variant ->
727→        def variantAssets = variant.mergeAssetsProvider.get().outputDir.get()
728→
729→        tasks.register("delete${variant.name.capitalize()}ReactNativeBundleSourceMap", Delete) {
730→            delete(fileTree(dir: variantAssets, includes: ['**/*.bundle.map']))
731→        }
732→
733→        tasks.register("copy${variant.name.capitalize()}ReactNativeBundleSourceMap", Copy) {
734→            from(variantAssets)
735→            into("${buildDir}/react-native-bundle-source-map")
736→            include("*.bundle", "*.bundle.map")
737→            finalizedBy("delete${variant.name.capitalize()}ReactNativeBundleSourceMap")
738→        }
739→
740→        variant.mergeAssetsProvider.configure {
741→            finalizedBy("copy${variant.name.capitalize()}ReactNativeBundleSourceMap")
742→        }
743→    }
744→}
745→

 16→import org.wordpress.android.fluxc.store.PostSchedulingNotificationStore.SchedulingReminderModel.Period.ONE_HOUR
 17→import org.wordpress.android.fluxc.store.PostSchedulingNotificationStore.SchedulingReminderModel.Period.TEN_MINUTES
 18→import org.wordpress.android.fluxc.store.PostSchedulingNotificationStore.SchedulingReminderModel.Period.WHEN_PUBLISHED
 19→import org.wordpress.android.fluxc.store.SiteStore
 20→import org.wordpress.android.ui.posts.EditPostRepository.UpdatePostResult

 22→import org.wordpress.android.util.LocaleManagerWrapper
 23→import org.wordpress.android.viewmodel.Event
 24→import org.wordpress.android.viewmodel.ResourceProvider
 25→import java.util.Calendar
 26→
 27→abstract class PublishSettingsViewModel
 28→constructor(
 29→    private val resourceProvider: ResourceProvider,
 30→    private val postSettingsUtils: PostSettingsUtils,
 31→    private val localeManagerWrapper: LocaleManagerWrapper,
 32→    private val postSchedulingNotificationStore: PostSchedulingNotificationStore,
 33→    private val siteStore: SiteStore
 34→) : ViewModel() {
 35→    protected var editPostRepository: EditPostRepository? = null
 36→        private set
 37→
 38→    var canPublishImmediately: Boolean = false
 39→
 40→    var year: Int? = null
 41→        private set
 42→    var month: Int? = null
 43→        private set
 44→    var day: Int? = null
 45→        private set
 46→    var hour: Int? = null
 47→        private set
 48→    var minute: Int? = null
 49→        private set
 50→
 51→    private val _onDatePicked = MutableLiveData<Event<Unit>>()
 52→    val onDatePicked: LiveData<Event<Unit>> = _onDatePicked
 53→    private val _onPublishedDateChanged = MutableLiveData<Event<Calendar>>()
 54→    val onPublishedDateChanged: LiveData<Event<Calendar>> = _onPublishedDateChanged
 55→    private val _onPostStatusChanged = MutableLiveData<PostStatus>()
 56→    val onPostStatusChanged: LiveData<PostStatus> = _onPostStatusChanged
 57→    private val _onUiModel = MutableLiveData<PublishUiModel>()
 58→    val onUiModel: LiveData<PublishUiModel> = _onUiModel
 59→
 60→    @Suppress("VariableNaming")
 61→    protected val _onToast = MutableLiveData<Event<String>>()
 62→    val onToast: LiveData<Event<String>> = _onToast
 63→    private val _onShowNotificationDialog = MutableLiveData<Event<Period?>>()
 64→    val onShowNotificationDialog: LiveData<Event<Period?>> = _onShowNotificationDialog
 65→    private val _onNotificationTime = MutableLiveData<Period?>()
 66→    val onNotificationTime: LiveData<Period?> = _onNotificationTime
 67→    private val _onNotificationAdded = MutableLiveData<Event<Notification>>()
 68→    val onNotificationAdded: LiveData<Event<Notification>> = _onNotificationAdded
 69→    private val _onAddToCalendar = MutableLiveData<Event<CalendarEvent>>()
 70→    val onAddToCalendar: LiveData<Event<CalendarEvent>> = _onAddToCalendar
 71→
 72→    open fun start(postRepository: EditPostRepository?) {
 73→        editPostRepository = postRepository
 74→        val startCalendar = postRepository?.let { getCurrentPublishDateAsCalendar(it) }
 75→            ?: localeManagerWrapper.getCurrentCalendar()
 76→        updateDateAndTimeFromCalendar(startCalendar)
 77→        onPostStatusChanged(postRepository?.getPost())
 78→    }
 79→
 80→    fun onPostStatusChanged(postModel: PostImmutableModel?) {
 81→        canPublishImmediately = postModel?.let {
 82→            PostUtils.shouldPublishImmediatelyOptionBeAvailable(
 83→                it.status
 84→            )
 85→        } ?: false
 86→        updateUiModel(postModel = postModel)
 87→    }
 88→
 89→    fun publishNow() {
 90→        val currentCalendar = localeManagerWrapper.getCurrentCalendar()
 91→        updateDateAndTimeFromCalendar(currentCalendar)
 92→        _onPublishedDateChanged.postValue(Event(currentCalendar))
 93→    }
 94→
 95→    fun onTimeSelected(selectedHour: Int, selectedMinute: Int) {
 96→        this.hour = selectedHour
 97→        this.minute = selectedMinute
 98→        val calendar = localeManagerWrapper.getCurrentCalendar()
 99→        calendar.set(year!!, month!!, day!!, hour!!, minute!!)
100→        _onPublishedDateChanged.postValue(Event(calendar))
101→    }
102→
103→    fun onDateSelected(year: Int, month: Int, dayOfMonth: Int) {
104→        this.year = year
105→        this.month = month
106→        this.day = dayOfMonth
107→        _onDatePicked.postValue(Event(Unit))
108→    }
109→
110→    fun onShowDialog(postModel: PostImmutableModel) {
111→        if (areNotificationsEnabled(postModel)) {
112→            val currentPeriod = postSchedulingNotificationStore.getSchedulingReminderPeriod(postModel.id)
113→            _onShowNotificationDialog.postValue(Event(currentPeriod))
114→        } else {
115→            _onToast.postValue(Event(resourceProvider.getString(R.string.post_notification_error)))
116→        }
117→    }
118→
119→    fun updatePost(updatedDate: Calendar, postRepository: EditPostRepository?) {
120→        postRepository?.updateAsync({ postModel ->
121→            val dateCreated = DateTimeUtils.iso8601FromDate(updatedDate.time)
122→            postModel.setDateCreated(dateCreated)
123→            val initialPostStatus = postRepository.status
124→            val isPublishDateInTheFuture = PostUtils.isPublishDateInTheFuture(dateCreated)
125→            var finalPostStatus = initialPostStatus
126→            if (initialPostStatus == DRAFT && isPublishDateInTheFuture) {
127→                // The previous logic was setting the status twice, once from draft to published and when the user
128→                // picked the time, it set it from published to scheduled. This is now done in one step.
129→                finalPostStatus = SCHEDULED
130→            } else if (initialPostStatus == PUBLISHED && postRepository.isLocalDraft) {
131→                // if user was changing dates for a local draft (not saved yet), only way to have it set to PUBLISH
132→                // is by running into the if case above. So, if they're updating the date again by calling
133→                // `updatePublishDate()`, get it back to DRAFT.
134→                finalPostStatus = DRAFT
135→            } else if (initialPostStatus == SCHEDULED && !isPublishDateInTheFuture) {
136→                // if this is a SCHEDULED post and the user is trying to Back-date it now, let's update it to DRAFT.
137→                // The other option was to make it published immediately but, let the user actively do that rather than
138→                // having the app be smart about it - we don't want to accidentally publish a post.
139→                finalPostStatus = DRAFT
140→                // show toast only once, when time is shown
141→                _onToast.postValue(Event(resourceProvider.getString(R.string.editor_post_converted_back_to_draft)))
142→            }
143→            postModel.setStatus(finalPostStatus.toString())
144→            _onPostStatusChanged.postValue(finalPostStatus)
145→            val scheduledTime = postSchedulingNotificationStore.getSchedulingReminderPeriod(postRepository.id)
146→            updateNotifications(postRepository, scheduledTime)
147→            true
148→        }, onCompleted = { postModel, result ->
149→            if (result == UpdatePostResult.Updated) {
150→                updateUiModel(postModel = postModel)
151→            }
152→        })
153→    }
154→
155→    fun updateUiModel(postModel: PostImmutableModel?) {
156→        if (postModel != null) {
157→            val notificationTime = postSchedulingNotificationStore.getSchedulingReminderPeriod(postModel.id)
158→            val publishDateLabel = postSettingsUtils.getPublishDateLabel(postModel)
159→            val now = localeManagerWrapper.getCurrentCalendar().timeInMillis - 10000
160→            val dateCreated = (DateTimeUtils.dateFromIso8601(postModel.dateCreated)
161→                ?: localeManagerWrapper.getCurrentCalendar().time).time
162→            val enableNotification = areNotificationsEnabled(postModel)
163→            val showNotification = dateCreated > now
164→            val notificationLabel = if (enableNotification && showNotification) {
165→                notificationTime.toLabel()
166→            } else {
167→                R.string.post_notification_off
168→            }
169→            _onUiModel.value = PublishUiModel(
170→                publishDateLabel,
171→                notificationLabel = notificationLabel,
172→                notificationEnabled = enableNotification,
173→                notificationVisible = showNotification
174→            )
175→        } else {
176→            _onUiModel.value = PublishUiModel(resourceProvider.getString(R.string.immediately))
177→        }
178→    }
179→
180→    fun onNotificationCreated(scheduleTime: Period?) {
181→        _onNotificationTime.value = scheduleTime
182→    }
183→
184→    fun scheduleNotification(postRepository: EditPostRepository, notificationTime: Period) {
185→        updateNotifications(postRepository, notificationTime)
186→        updateUiModel(postRepository.getPost())
187→    }
188→
189→    fun onAddToCalendar(postRepository: EditPostRepository) {
190→        val startTime = DateTimeUtils.dateFromIso8601(postRepository.dateCreated).time
191→        val site = siteStore.getSiteByLocalId(postRepository.localSiteId)
192→        val title = resourceProvider.getString(
193→            R.string.calendar_scheduled_post_title,
194→            postRepository.title
195→        )
196→        val appName = resourceProvider.getString(R.string.app_name)
197→        val description = resourceProvider.getString(
198→            R.string.calendar_scheduled_post_description,
199→            postRepository.title,
200→            site?.name ?: site?.url ?: "",
201→            appName,
202→            postRepository.link
203→        )
204→        _onAddToCalendar.value = Event(CalendarEvent(title, description, startTime))
205→    }
206→
207→    private fun getCurrentPublishDateAsCalendar(postRepository: EditPostRepository): Calendar {
208→        val calendar = localeManagerWrapper.getCurrentCalendar()
209→        if (postRepository.getPost() != null) {
210→            val dateCreated = postRepository.dateCreated
211→            // Set the currently selected time if available
212→            if (!TextUtils.isEmpty(dateCreated)) {
213→                calendar.time = DateTimeUtils.dateFromIso8601(dateCreated)
214→                calendar.timeZone = localeManagerWrapper.getTimeZone()
215→            }
216→        }
217→        return calendar
218→    }
219→
220→    private fun updateNotifications(
221→        postRepository: EditPostRepository,
222→        schedulingReminderPeriod: Period = OFF
223→    ) {
224→        postSchedulingNotificationStore.deleteSchedulingReminders(postRepository.id)
225→        if (schedulingReminderPeriod != OFF) {
226→            val notificationId = postSchedulingNotificationStore.schedule(postRepository.id, schedulingReminderPeriod)
227→            val scheduledCalendar = localeManagerWrapper.getCurrentCalendar().apply {
228→                timeInMillis = System.currentTimeMillis()
229→                time = DateTimeUtils.dateFromIso8601(postRepository.dateCreated)
230→                val scheduledMinutes = when (schedulingReminderPeriod) {
231→                    ONE_HOUR -> -60
232→                    TEN_MINUTES -> -10
233→                    WHEN_PUBLISHED -> 0
234→                    OFF -> return
235→                }
236→                add(Calendar.MINUTE, scheduledMinutes)
237→            }
238→            if (scheduledCalendar.after(localeManagerWrapper.getCurrentCalendar())) {
239→                notificationId?.let {
240→                    _onNotificationAdded.postValue(Event(Notification(notificationId, scheduledCalendar.timeInMillis)))
241→                }
242→            }
243→        }
244→    }
245→
246→    private fun areNotificationsEnabled(postModel: PostImmutableModel): Boolean {
247→        val futureTime = localeManagerWrapper.getCurrentCalendar().timeInMillis + 6000
248→        val dateCreated = (DateTimeUtils.dateFromIso8601(postModel.dateCreated)
249→            ?: localeManagerWrapper.getCurrentCalendar().time).time
250→        return dateCreated > futureTime
251→    }
252→
253→    private fun Period.toLabel(): Int {
254→        return when (this) {
255→            OFF -> R.string.post_notification_off
256→            ONE_HOUR -> R.string.post_notification_one_hour_before
257→            TEN_MINUTES -> R.string.post_notification_ten_minutes_before
258→            WHEN_PUBLISHED -> R.string.post_notification_when_published
259→        }
260→    }
261→
262→    private fun updateDateAndTimeFromCalendar(startCalendar: Calendar) {
263→        year = startCalendar.get(Calendar.YEAR)
264→        month = startCalendar.get(Calendar.MONTH)
265→        day = startCalendar.get(Calendar.DAY_OF_MONTH)
266→        hour = startCalendar.get(Calendar.HOUR_OF_DAY)
267→        minute = startCalendar.get(Calendar.MINUTE)
268→    }
269→
270→    data class PublishUiModel(
271→        val publishDateLabel: String,
272→        val notificationLabel: Int = R.string.post_notification_off,
273→        val notificationEnabled: Boolean = false,
274→        val notificationVisible: Boolean = true
275→    )
276→
277→    data class Notification(val id: Int, val scheduledTime: Long)
278→
279→    data class CalendarEvent(val title: String, val description: String, val startTime: Long)
280→}
281→

 61→    protected val _onToast = MutableLiveData<Event<String>>()
 62→    val onToast: LiveData<Event<String>> = _onToast
 63→    private val _onShowNotificationDialog = MutableLiveData<Event<Period?>>()
 64→    val onShowNotificationDialog: LiveData<Event<Period?>> = _onShowNotificationDialog
 65→    private val _onNotificationTime = MutableLiveData<Period?>()
 66→    val onNotificationTime: LiveData<Period?> = _onNotificationTime
 67→    private val _onNotificationAdded = MutableLiveData<Event<Notification>>()
 68→    val onNotificationAdded: LiveData<Event<Notification>> = _onNotificationAdded
 69→    private val _onAddToCalendar = MutableLiveData<Event<CalendarEvent>>()
 70→    val onAddToCalendar: LiveData<Event<CalendarEvent>> = _onAddToCalendar
 71→
 72→    open fun start(postRepository: EditPostRepository?) {
 73→        editPostRepository = postRepository
 74→        val startCalendar = postRepository?.let { getCurrentPublishDateAsCalendar(it) }
 75→            ?: localeManagerWrapper.getCurrentCalendar()
 76→        updateDateAndTimeFromCalendar(startCalendar)
 77→        onPostStatusChanged(postRepository?.getPost())
 78→    }
 79→
 80→    fun onPostStatusChanged(postModel: PostImmutableModel?) {
 81→        canPublishImmediately = postModel?.let {
 82→            PostUtils.shouldPublishImmediatelyOptionBeAvailable(
 83→                it.status
 84→            )
 85→        } ?: false
 86→        updateUiModel(postModel = postModel)
 87→    }
 88→
 89→    fun publishNow() {
 90→        val currentCalendar = localeManagerWrapper.getCurrentCalendar()
 91→        updateDateAndTimeFromCalendar(currentCalendar)
 92→        _onPublishedDateChanged.postValue(Event(currentCalendar))
 93→    }
 94→
 95→    fun onTimeSelected(selectedHour: Int, selectedMinute: Int) {
 96→        this.hour = selectedHour
 97→        this.minute = selectedMinute
 98→        val calendar = localeManagerWrapper.getCurrentCalendar()
 99→        calendar.set(year!!, month!!, day!!, hour!!, minute!!)
100→        _onPublishedDateChanged.postValue(Event(calendar))
101→    }
102→
103→    fun onDateSelected(year: Int, month: Int, dayOfMonth: Int) {
104→        this.year = year
105→        this.month = month
106→        this.day = dayOfMonth
107→        _onDatePicked.postValue(Event(Unit))
108→    }
109→
110→    fun onShowDialog(postModel: PostImmutableModel) {
111→        if (areNotificationsEnabled(postModel)) {
112→            val currentPeriod = postSchedulingNotificationStore.getSchedulingReminderPeriod(postModel.id)
113→            _onShowNotificationDialog.postValue(Event(currentPeriod))
114→        } else {
115→            _onToast.postValue(Event(resourceProvider.getString(R.string.post_notification_error)))
116→        }
117→    }
118→
119→    fun updatePost(updatedDate: Calendar, postRepository: EditPostRepository?) {
120→        postRepository?.updateAsync({ postModel ->
121→            val dateCreated = DateTimeUtils.iso8601FromDate(updatedDate.time)
122→            postModel.setDateCreated(dateCreated)
123→            val initialPostStatus = postRepository.status
124→            val isPublishDateInTheFuture = PostUtils.isPublishDateInTheFuture(dateCreated)
125→            var finalPostStatus = initialPostStatus
126→            if (initialPostStatus == DRAFT && isPublishDateInTheFuture) {
127→                // The previous logic was setting the status twice, once from draft to published and when the user
128→                // picked the time, it set it from published to scheduled. This is now done in one step.
129→                finalPostStatus = SCHEDULED
130→            } else if (initialPostStatus == PUBLISHED && postRepository.isLocalDraft) {
131→                // if user was changing dates for a local draft (not saved yet), only way to have it set to PUBLISH
132→                // is by running into the if case above. So, if they're updating the date again by calling
133→                // `updatePublishDate()`, get it back to DRAFT.
134→                finalPostStatus = DRAFT
135→            } else if (initialPostStatus == SCHEDULED && !isPublishDateInTheFuture) {
136→                // if this is a SCHEDULED post and the user is trying to Back-date it now, let's update it to DRAFT.
137→                // The other option was to make it published immediately but, let the user actively do that rather than
138→                // having the app be smart about it - we don't want to accidentally publish a post.
139→                finalPostStatus = DRAFT
140→                // show toast only once, when time is shown
141→                _onToast.postValue(Event(resourceProvider.getString(R.string.editor_post_converted_back_to_draft)))
142→            }
143→            postModel.setStatus(finalPostStatus.toString())
144→            _onPostStatusChanged.postValue(finalPostStatus)
145→            val scheduledTime = postSchedulingNotificationStore.getSchedulingReminderPeriod(postRepository.id)
146→            updateNotifications(postRepository, scheduledTime)
147→            true
148→        }, onCompleted = { postModel, result ->
149→            if (result == UpdatePostResult.Updated) {
150→                updateUiModel(postModel = postModel)
151→            }
152→        })
153→    }
154→
155→    fun updateUiModel(postModel: PostImmutableModel?) {
156→        if (postModel != null) {
157→            val notificationTime = postSchedulingNotificationStore.getSchedulingReminderPeriod(postModel.id)
158→            val publishDateLabel = postSettingsUtils.getPublishDateLabel(postModel)
159→            val now = localeManagerWrapper.getCurrentCalendar().timeInMillis - 10000
160→            val dateCreated = (DateTimeUtils.dateFromIso8601(postModel.dateCreated)
161→                ?: localeManagerWrapper.getCurrentCalendar().time).time
162→            val enableNotification = areNotificationsEnabled(postModel)
163→            val showNotification = dateCreated > now
164→            val notificationLabel = if (enableNotification && showNotification) {
165→                notificationTime.toLabel()
166→            } else {
167→                R.string.post_notification_off
168→            }
169→            _onUiModel.value = PublishUiModel(
170→                publishDateLabel,
171→                notificationLabel = notificationLabel,
172→                notificationEnabled = enableNotification,
173→                notificationVisible = showNotification
174→            )
175→        } else {
176→            _onUiModel.value = PublishUiModel(resourceProvider.getString(R.string.immediately))
177→        }
178→    }
179→
180→    fun onNotificationCreated(scheduleTime: Period?) {
181→        _onNotificationTime.value = scheduleTime
182→    }
183→
184→    fun scheduleNotification(postRepository: EditPostRepository, notificationTime: Period) {
185→        updateNotifications(postRepository, notificationTime)
186→        updateUiModel(postRepository.getPost())
187→    }
188→
189→    fun onAddToCalendar(postRepository: EditPostRepository) {
190→        val startTime = DateTimeUtils.dateFromIso8601(postRepository.dateCreated).time
191→        val site = siteStore.getSiteByLocalId(postRepository.localSiteId)
192→        val title = resourceProvider.getString(
193→            R.string.calendar_scheduled_post_title,
194→            postRepository.title
195→        )
196→        val appName = resourceProvider.getString(R.string.app_name)
197→        val description = resourceProvider.getString(
198→            R.string.calendar_scheduled_post_description,
199→            postRepository.title,
200→            site?.name ?: site?.url ?: "",
201→            appName,
202→            postRepository.link
203→        )
204→        _onAddToCalendar.value = Event(CalendarEvent(title, description, startTime))
205→    }
206→
207→    private fun getCurrentPublishDateAsCalendar(postRepository: EditPostRepository): Calendar {
208→        val calendar = localeManagerWrapper.getCurrentCalendar()
209→        val dateCreated = postRepository.dateCreated
210→        // Set the currently selected time if available
211→        if (!TextUtils.isEmpty(dateCreated)) {
212→            calendar.time = DateTimeUtils.dateFromIso8601(dateCreated)
213→            calendar.timeZone = localeManagerWrapper.getTimeZone()
214→        }
215→        return calendar
216→    }
217→
218→    private fun updateNotifications(
219→        postRepository: EditPostRepository,
220→        schedulingReminderPeriod: Period = OFF
221→    ) {
222→        postSchedulingNotificationStore.deleteSchedulingReminders(postRepository.id)
223→        if (schedulingReminderPeriod != OFF) {
224→            val notificationId = postSchedulingNotificationStore.schedule(postRepository.id, schedulingReminderPeriod)
225→            val scheduledCalendar = localeManagerWrapper.getCurrentCalendar().apply {
226→                timeInMillis = System.currentTimeMillis()
227→                time = DateTimeUtils.dateFromIso8601(postRepository.dateCreated)
228→                val scheduledMinutes = when (schedulingReminderPeriod) {
229→                    ONE_HOUR -> -60
230→                    TEN_MINUTES -> -10
231→                    WHEN_PUBLISHED -> 0
232→                    OFF -> return
233→                }
234→                add(Calendar.MINUTE, scheduledMinutes)
235→            }
236→            if (scheduledCalendar.after(localeManagerWrapper.getCurrentCalendar())) {
237→                notificationId?.let {
238→                    _onNotificationAdded.postValue(Event(Notification(notificationId, scheduledCalendar.timeInMillis)))
239→                }
240→            }
241→        }
242→    }
243→
244→    private fun areNotificationsEnabled(postModel: PostImmutableModel): Boolean {
245→        val futureTime = localeManagerWrapper.getCurrentCalendar().timeInMillis + 6000
246→        val dateCreated = (DateTimeUtils.dateFromIso8601(postModel.dateCreated)
247→            ?: localeManagerWrapper.getCurrentCalendar().time).time
248→        return dateCreated > futureTime
249→    }
250→
251→    private fun Period.toLabel(): Int {
252→        return when (this) {
253→            OFF -> R.string.post_notification_off
254→            ONE_HOUR -> R.string.post_notification_one_hour_before
255→            TEN_MINUTES -> R.string.post_notification_ten_minutes_before
256→            WHEN_PUBLISHED -> R.string.post_notification_when_published
257→        }
258→    }
259→
260→    private fun updateDateAndTimeFromCalendar(startCalendar: Calendar) {
261→        year = startCalendar.get(Calendar.YEAR)
262→        month = startCalendar.get(Calendar.MONTH)
263→        day = startCalendar.get(Calendar.DAY_OF_MONTH)
264→        hour = startCalendar.get(Calendar.HOUR_OF_DAY)
265→        minute = startCalendar.get(Calendar.MINUTE)
266→    }
267→
268→    data class PublishUiModel(
269→        val publishDateLabel: String,
270→        val notificationLabel: Int = R.string.post_notification_off,
271→        val notificationEnabled: Boolean = false,
272→        val notificationVisible: Boolean = true
273→    )
274→
275→    data class Notification(val id: Int, val scheduledTime: Long)
276→
277→    data class CalendarEvent(val title: String, val description: String, val startTime: Long)
278→}

1→package org.wordpress.android.ui.posts
 2→
 3→import androidx.lifecycle.LiveData
 4→import androidx.lifecycle.MutableLiveData
 5→import org.wordpress.android.R
 6→import org.wordpress.android.fluxc.model.SiteModel
 7→import org.wordpress.android.fluxc.store.PostSchedulingNotificationStore
 8→import org.wordpress.android.fluxc.store.SiteStore
 9→import org.wordpress.android.models.Person
10→import org.wordpress.android.ui.people.utils.PeopleUtils.FetchUsersCallback
11→import org.wordpress.android.ui.people.utils.PeopleUtilsWrapper
12→import org.wordpress.android.util.LocaleManagerWrapper
13→import org.wordpress.android.viewmodel.Event
14→import org.wordpress.android.viewmodel.ResourceProvider
15→import javax.inject.Inject
16→
17→class EditPostPublishSettingsViewModel @Inject constructor(
18→    private val resourceProvider: ResourceProvider,
19→    postSettingsUtils: PostSettingsUtils,
20→    private val peopleUtilsWrapper: PeopleUtilsWrapper,
21→    localeManagerWrapper: LocaleManagerWrapper,
22→    postSchedulingNotificationStore: PostSchedulingNotificationStore,
23→    private val siteStore: SiteStore,
24→) : PublishSettingsViewModel(
25→    resourceProvider,
26→    postSettingsUtils,
27→    localeManagerWrapper,
28→    postSchedulingNotificationStore,
29→    siteStore
30→) {
31→    private val _authors = MutableLiveData<List<Person>>()
32→    val authors: LiveData<List<Person>> = _authors
33→
34→    // Used for combining fetched users
35→    private val fetchedAuthors = mutableListOf<Person>()
36→
37→    private var isStarted = false
38→
39→    override fun start(postRepository: EditPostRepository?) {
40→        super.start(postRepository)
41→        if (isStarted) return
42→        isStarted = true
43→
44→        val siteModel = postRepository?.localSiteId?.let {
45→            siteStore.getSiteByLocalId(it)
46→        } ?: return
47→
48→        if (siteModel.hasCapabilityListUsers) {
49→            fetchAuthors(siteModel)
50→        }
51→    }
52→
53→    // This fetches authors page by page and combine the result in fetchedAuthors.
54→    private fun fetchAuthors(site: SiteModel) {
55→        peopleUtilsWrapper.fetchAuthors(site, fetchedAuthors.size, object : FetchUsersCallback {
56→            override fun onSuccess(peopleList: List<Person>, isEndOfList: Boolean) {
57→                fetchedAuthors.addAll(peopleList)
58→                if (isEndOfList) {
59→                    _authors.value = fetchedAuthors
60→                } else {
61→                    fetchAuthors(site)
62→                }
63→            }
64→
65→            override fun onError() {
66→                _onToast.postValue(Event(resourceProvider.getString(R.string.error_fetch_authors_list)))
67→            }
68→        })
69→    }
70→
71→    fun getAuthorIndex(authorId: Long) = authors.value?.indexOfFirst { it.personID == authorId } ?: -1
72→}

218→            // Limit string resources to Mag16 only for Jetpack

220→            resourceConfigurations = ["ar", "de", "es", "fr", "he", "id", "it", "ja", "ko", "nl", "pt-rBR", "ru", "sv", "tr", "zh-rCN", "zh-rTW"]
221→            // Note 2: `in` is the legacy locale code for `id` and `iw` is the legacy locale code for `he`.

324→/// Dynamically add `buildConfigFields` on a given variant/flavor from prefixed properties
325→/// (This is used to e.g. add every property prefixed `wp.` in `gradle.properties` as a BuildConfigField in the `wordpress` flavor)
326→///
327→/// `prefixes` is an array of prefixes to search for, without their `.` dot (e.g. `["wp", "jp"]`).
328→/// Properties found with prefixes first in that array will be overridden by the ones found with prefixes later in the array.
329→static def addBuildConfigFieldsFromPrefixedProperties(variant, properties, prefixes) {
330→    // Build a Map of prefixed properties found. Keys in this Map will be lowercased and `.` replaced by `_`
331→    def fields_map = [:]
332→    prefixes.each { prefix ->
333→        properties.each { property ->
334→            if (property.key.toLowerCase().startsWith("${prefix.toLowerCase()}.")) {
335→                def key = property.key.toLowerCase().replace("${prefix.toLowerCase()}.", "").replace(".", "_")
336→                fields_map[key] = property.value
337→            }
338→        }
339→    }
340→
341→    // Then define the found properties as buildConfigFields
342→    fields_map.each {
343→        variant.buildConfigField "String", it.key.toUpperCase(), "\"${it.value}\""
344→    }
345→}

1→package org.wordpress.android.ui.posts

  9→import org.wordpress.android.fluxc.model.post.PostStatus
 10→import org.wordpress.android.fluxc.model.post.PostStatus.DRAFT
 11→import org.wordpress.android.fluxc.model.post.PostStatus.PUBLISHED
 12→import org.wordpress.android.fluxc.model.post.PostStatus.SCHEDULED
 13→import org.wordpress.android.fluxc.store.PostSchedulingNotificationStore
 14→import org.wordpress.android.fluxc.store.PostSchedulingNotificationStore.SchedulingReminderModel.Period
 15→import org.wordpress.android.fluxc.store.PostSchedulingNotificationStore.SchedulingReminderModel.Period.OFF
 16→import org.wordpress.android.fluxc.store.PostSchedulingNotificationStore.SchedulingReminderModel.Period.ONE_HOUR
 17→import org.wordpress.android.fluxc.store.PostSchedulingNotificationStore.SchedulingReminderModel.Period.TEN_MINUTES
 18→import org.wordpress.android.fluxc.store.PostSchedulingNotificationStore.SchedulingReminderModel.Period.WHEN_PUBLISHED

 20→import org.wordpress.android.ui.posts.EditPostRepository.UpdatePostResult

 24→import org.wordpress.android.viewmodel.ResourceProvider
